leagueBriefing: It was a night of spoilers in the Eastern Conference as two road teams pulled off clean-sheet upsets. CF Montréal silenced the Red Bulls with a clinical performance led by a former New York man, while Toronto FC found a late spark in Cincinnati to secure their first three points of the season. For both home sides, it was a frustrating lesson in efficiency as they failed to turn possession and shot volume into results.

games.headline: Wiki Carmona haunts former club as Montréal stuns Red Bulls in Harrison
games.score: New York Red Bulls 0 - 3 CF Montréal
games.brief: CF Montréal pulled off the shock of the weekend, walking into Red Bull Arena and dismantling a New York side that had started the season perfect. The story was all about Wiki Carmona, the former Red Bull midfielder who looked right at home on his old stomping grounds, netting twice to lead the rout. Montréal had struggled mightily in their first two matches, but they found their rhythm early with a Prince Owusu penalty and never looked back, handing New York their first loss of the 2026 campaign.
bullets: Wiki Carmona scored a brace against his former team, including a 44th-minute free kick that caught Ethan Horvath off guard.
Prince Owusu opened the scoring in the 8th minute with a penalty, marking Montréal's first goal of the 2026 season.
New York dominated the ball with 67.1% possession in the first half but failed to convert 17-year-old Julian Hall's chances.
The victory ended a 17-match streak without a clean sheet for CF Montréal, dating back to June 2025.
Thomas Gillier stood tall for the visitors, making five saves to preserve the shutout.
games.context: The result marks New York Red Bulls' first loss of the season and snaps Montréal's winless start.

highlights.title: New York Red Bulls vs. CF Montréal | Full Match Highlights

games.headline: Sallói strikes late to give Toronto FC gritty road win in Cincinnati
games.brief: Toronto FC finally found the win column in 2026, using a smash-and-grab strategy to take down FC Cincinnati at TQL Stadium. In a match defined by physical play and defensive grit, Hungarian international Dániel Sallói proved to be the difference-maker, slotting home a rebound in the 86th minute. Cincinnati will be left wondering how they came away empty-handed after firing 15 shots, but Toronto’s backline held firm to earn a vital three points on the road.
bullets: Dániel Sallói scored his first goal of the season in the 86th minute, pouncing on a loose ball after a Richie Laryea run.
Toronto FC’s defense recorded 19 clearances to withstand a heavy Cincinnati attack that held 55.8% possession.
Luka Gavran earned the clean sheet for Toronto, highlighted by a crucial 67th-minute save on Pavel Bucha.
The match was a physical affair featuring 25 total fouls and four yellow cards.
This win marks Toronto's first goal and victory in Cincinnati since the 2020 season.
games.context: Toronto FC secures its first points of the year while Cincinnati suffers a second consecutive scoreless outing.
